title: Too Long
description: Shimamura reflects on Adachi's transparent, untouched nature. Adachi reflects on Shimamura as the ship that carried her to another world. Two sides of the same story—one measured, one burning.
content:
Shimamura's Side:

If you asked me what kind of person Adachi is… I'd say she's transparent. She's always seemed like someone living in a world untouched by anything around her. Not exactly isolated—just… untouched.

Her connections with others have always been thin. She's never really been shaped by anyone.

That includes friends—and even family. Because of that distance, Adachi has kept a kind of innocent clarity. Calling her 'unpolished' feels a little silly—silly for her, and maybe even for me to say out loud.

But it's true. She has parts of herself that are completely untouched. And the thought of that clear, quiet self being colored by me… it almost feels wrong, like wasting something beautiful.

And yet… she still comes to my side. She stands beside me. She looks at me sincerely.

And I don't mind it. Not at all.

To me, that's the kind of person Adachi is… Though if I sound like I'm speaking with authority here, the truth is that I actually haven't known her for very long.

It was the first year of high school, just as summer was fading.

I tumbled into that small, stifling world—escaping from the heat—and there she was.

The time we've spent together since then isn't long. Which means my impression of her could easily be overturned someday. All we'd need is a bigger place, more chances to talk, to share… and maybe everything would shift.

Maybe it will. But even just like this, I want to keep walking beside her.

That's all.

Adachi's Side:

To me, Shimamura is like a ship—one that carries me into another world.

Whether she means to or not.

After meeting Shimamura, the world around me completely changed. It was like she took me to a land that had nothing in common with the place I used to live. Before I knew it, I had been dropped into a world where nothing existed except her.

At first, I didn't know what to do. but now… now I'm doing everything I can just to stay here.

I feel no attachment to the place I used to be. I barely even remember it. It feels like something from a hundred, two hundred years ago—far too distant for nostalgia. There's nothing there worth holding onto.

Now there is only Shimamura. Shimamura is the only one.

Somewhere in the invisible air, in the unreachable sky, beyond the horizon no one can ever reach—if there's something that truly exists there, it can only be Shimamura.

What is it about Shimamura that drives me to this point? I think about it sometimes. No—'sometimes' is wrong. It's every day. Every moment. I hardly use my brain for anything that isn't Shimamura anymore. She fills my lungs, all the way to the very bottom.

If I had to list her charms, the first thing is simple: She just feels good to be around.

It sounds vague, but that 'air-like presence' of hers knocks the wind out of me. It bewilders me, bewitches me, unravels everything in my head. Just seeing her calm face, her hair swaying in the wind, makes all the veins in my body scream.

Her balance is perfect—just by being in my sight, she fills every gap in my world. I can't explain it well; I only know she pierces right through me.

'Pierces' is exactly the word. It feels like someone shoved all the air in the world straight into my stomach in one breath.

Do other people not feel this when they look at her? I've never seen anyone else's eyes spin the way mine do around her. But maybe that's only because I've barely gotten to watch her around others. If so, that would be terrible. I want this feeling to be mine alone.

I have so many hopes, fears, dreads, and desires for Shimamura. All of these emotions tear me apart in every direction. They probably represent who I really am now.

Anyway—Shimamura is wonderful. Just a single glance floods me with emotions I never even knew existed. They're like a poison that numbs me until even my fingertips lose feeling.

Why does Shimamura do this to me? Whenever I think about her voice or her smile in my room, my thoughts scatter into embarrassment.

I can't answer the question. I get tired just trying. Is Shimamura good for my health? Definitely not.

Because everything inside me burns as though I'm destroying myself. But even burning doesn't stop me.

Shimamura gives me life.

She keeps my unhealthy body moving.

She gives meaning to my routine steps between home and school.

She makes even walking feel worth something.

My blood runs hotter every time I think of her. It feels like I'm compressing decades of life into a single moment. So I probably won't live long. If I did, it would be like living hundreds of years compared to everyone else—busy, consuming myself, burning out.

Even though I feel that way, I'm sure Shimamura hasn't thought about any of this. To her, I'm probably just a slightly weird friend. Our feelings aren't equal. Now, and probably forever.

That's painful. I'm not kind enough to accept it. Just standing beside her, just admiring her, will never satisfy me. My personality isn't that good. Honestly, I'm a bad person. So being called a delinquent for skipping class doesn't bother me at all.

I've started to realize recently that I'm an aggressive person. I'm full of thorns. Those thorns kept people away, kept me comfortably alone before I crossed into a new world.

But now they're a problem.

I can't pull them out. I can't break them off.

They're part of me—just like fish can't live out of water, birds are born with wings, and humans can't understand other animals' languages. There are things we can change, and things we never can.

So now I'm trying to touch Shimamura with those thorns. Carefully. Slowly. Terrified of hurting her, but unable to stop myself.

Sometimes I do hurt her. And when that happens, I regret it so much I could cry. But even then, I still reach out with my thorns. Because wanting to touch her pushes me forward, even if I'm too childish to think of others.

One day—one day, the thorns won't hurt her. She'll notice my touch and smile back at me. When that day comes, my whole body will warp with happiness and dizziness.

Ah… Shimamura really is bad for my health. But I have no choice except to run toward her.

I hope that someday, Shimamura will feel my world too. That her world will include me. That mine will include her. Two worlds getting so close that they almost collide and destroy each other—in that fleeting moment where each world floats above the other's sky.

Why am I drawn to Shimamura? I still don't know. Maybe it's something we were born with—genes, fate, or maybe the laws of the universe.

Whatever the reason, it lies too deep for me to ever understand.

The only thing I do know is what resides beyond that unclear boundary line: What I long for.

I just want to stay in the world I desire—right beside the one thing in it that is unmistakably certain.

Like a prayer, that is what I wish for. That's all.
